page: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a dedicated virtual server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and the GPU count is listed as 0.0. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the inf2.8xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2991.39, placing it in the top 25%. Its multi-core performance is average, scoring 47851.71 in stress-ng and 13860.0 in Geekbench. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 37952.63, also in the top 25%. Memory performance is solid, highlighted by a PassMark memory score of 2877.72, though large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier at 78.67 GB/sec and 44.64 GB/sec respectively.

Despite the lack of discrete GPUs, the instance shows strong performance in text generation for small language models with 135M parameters, achieving 256.61 tokens per second. However, performance drops significantly for larger models, with prompt processing for a 70B parameter model registering a weak 7.08 tokens per second. Given its balanced resource allocation, strong single-core execution, and high-speed memory, the inf2.8xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, cryptographic operations, medium-scale web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that do not require dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based virtual server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage or discrete GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities and high cryptographic throughput, alongside a strong PassMark memory score. While it excels at text generation for smaller language models, performance degrades on larger models. This instance is highly suitable for general-purpose applications, cryptographic tasks, and moderate-scale web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, and 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, offering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and high-speed DDR4 memory for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor
128 GB of DDR4 memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Optimized for general-purpose computing, web serving, and light inference
